title: Sedgefield water alert: Tap water not safe for consumption due to salinity levels
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"SEDGEFIELD NEWS - Residents of Sedgefield are urged to refrain from drinking tap water as the town’s potable water is currently not fit for human consumption.","html":"SEDGEFIELD NEWS - Residents of Sedgefield are urged to refrain from drinking tap water as the town’s potable water is currently not fit for human consumption."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Knysna Municipality issued a public notice stating that recent testing revealed elevated levels of conductivity and salinity in the water supply, making it unsafe to drink."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"However, the water may still be used for other domestic purposes.","html":"However, the water may still be used for other domestic purposes."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“We are actively addressing the situation and are committed to resolving it as swiftly as possible,” the municipality said.","html":"“We are actively addressing the situation and are committed to resolving it as swiftly as possible,” the municipality said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"To alleviate the immediate impact on residents, the municipality has dispatched water tankers to three key locations in Sedgefield:","html":"To alleviate the immediate impact on residents, the municipality has dispatched water tankers to three key locations in Sedgefield:"},{"type":"list","items":["Smutsville","Groenvlei","Sedgefield Town"],"ordered":false},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The water tankers already operated until 22:00 last night and resumed distribution from 06:00 today, 22 October.","html":"The water tankers already operated until 22:00 last night and resumed distribution from 06:00 today, 22 October."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Each area will be serviced by one water truck as part of a temporary arrangement until the drinking water quality is restored.","html":"Each area will be serviced by one water truck as part of a temporary arrangement until the drinking water quality is restored."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Knysna-Plett Herald has sent an official inquiry to the municipality as to the cause of the elevated conductivity and salinity levels."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"This is a developing story and updates will be published as more information becomes available.","html":"This is a developing story and updates will be published as more information becomes available."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Sedgefield water alert: Tap water not safe for consumption due to salinity levels
